pages.title: Landing Page
pages.description: Marketing homepage for OpenBat — an AI chatbot analytics platform described as 'Google Analytics for AI chatbots'. Features a hero section, animated chat widget demo showing conversation analysis, AI provider logos carousel, feature sections (conversation analysis, sentiment scoring, topic detection, workflow automation), code integration examples (Vercel AI SDK, TypeScript, Python, REST API), pricing tiers (Free through Enterprise), FAQs accordion, testimonials, and a CTA footer.
elements: button: Feature Gap Risk (feature demo toggle)
tablist: Code examples (Vercel AI SDK, TypeScript, Python, REST API)

flows.name: Navigate to sign up from landing page
flows.description: Start the account creation process from the marketing homepage. Users can either click 'Sign Up' in the nav bar or 'Start Free' / 'Get Started' buttons throughout the page.
flows.persona: Prospective user who has read about OpenBat and wants to create a free account to try the product
preconditions: User is on the landing page
steps: Visit the landing page at /
Click 'Sign Up' in the top navigation bar, or click any 'Start Free' or 'Get Started' button
User is redirected to /auth/sign-up
flows.expectedOutcome: User lands on the sign-up page ready to create an account
failureModes: Links might be placeholder (#link) and not navigate

flows.name: Explore feature demos
flows.description: Interactive feature demonstration on the landing page showing how OpenBat detects different conversation analysis patterns like missed upsells, feature gap risks, and bot hallucinations.
flows.persona: Prospective customer evaluating the product before signing up
preconditions: User is on the landing page
steps: Scroll to the 'From raw conversation to actionable insight' section
Click 'Missed Upsell', 'Feature Gap Risk', or 'Bot Hallucination' buttons to toggle between demo scenarios
View the animated conversation example, prompt suggestion, and Slack alert preview
flows.expectedOutcome: User sees different AI analysis scenarios and understands the product value proposition
failureModes: Animations may not load on slow connections
relatedFlows: Navigate to sign up from landing page

pages.title: Login
pages.description: Authentication page for existing users to log into their OpenBat account. Supports email/password login and social authentication via Apple, Google, and Meta. Features a split layout with login form on the left and decorative image on the right.

flows.name: Email login
flows.description: Authenticate using email and password credentials to access the main dashboard. This is the primary authentication method for existing users.
flows.persona: Returning user who already has an account and wants to access their dashboard
preconditions: User is not already logged in
User knows their email and password
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Enter email address in the Email text field
Enter password in the Password text field
Click the 'Login' button to submit credentials
Wait for authentication to complete and redirect to the dashboard
flows.expectedOutcome: User is redirected to the main dashboard (/platform) showing their chatbots and organization data
failureModes: Invalid email or password shows an error toast
Account may not exist

flows.name: Social auth login
flows.description: Log in using a third-party social authentication provider (Apple, Google, or Meta) instead of email/password.
flows.persona: User who prefers social login or has forgotten their password
preconditions: User has linked their social account or wants to create an account via social auth
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Click one of the social auth buttons (Apple, Google, or Meta)
Complete the OAuth flow in the provider's popup/redirect
Return to OpenBat and be redirected to the dashboard
flows.expectedOutcome: User is authenticated and redirected to /platform
failureModes: OAuth popup blocked by browser
Social account not linked to any OpenBat account

flows.name: Forgot password flow
flows.description: Initiate a password reset by navigating to the forgot password page from the login form.
flows.persona: User who has forgotten their password and cannot log in
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Click the 'Forgot your password?' link
User is redirected to /auth/forgot-password
flows.expectedOutcome: User lands on the password reset page to enter their email

pages.title: Sign Up
pages.description: Account creation page for new users. Requires email, password, and password confirmation. Also supports social auth sign-up via Apple, Google, and Meta. Has a link back to the login page for existing users.

flows.name: Email sign up
flows.description: Create a new OpenBat account using email and password. Password must be at least 8 characters long and must match the confirmation field.
flows.persona: New user who wants to create an account to start analyzing their AI chatbot conversations
preconditions: User does not have an existing account
steps: Navigate to /auth/sign-up
Enter email address in the Email field
Enter password in the Password field (minimum 8 characters)
Re-enter password in the Confirm Password field
Click 'Create Account'
Complete any email verification if required
flows.expectedOutcome: Account is created and user is redirected to the platform, likely to create their first organization
failureModes: Password too short (must be 8+ characters)
Passwords don't match
Email already in use

pages.title: Reset Your Password
pages.description: Password reset page where users enter their email to receive a password reset link. Simple form with a single email field and a link back to the login page.

flows.name: Password reset
flows.description: Request a password reset email to regain access to an account when the user has forgotten their password.
flows.persona: User who has forgotten their password
preconditions: User has an existing account with a valid email
steps: Navigate to /auth/forgot-password (or click 'Forgot your password?' from login page)
Enter the account email address in the Email field
Click 'Send reset email'
Check email inbox for the reset link
Follow the link to set a new password
flows.expectedOutcome: A password reset email is sent to the user's address with a link to create a new password
failureModes: Email not found in system
Reset email goes to spam
Reset link expires

pages.title: Dashboard — Chatbots
pages.description: Main authenticated dashboard showing all chatbots in the current organization. Chatbots are displayed as cards (or list view) with name, API key prefix, creator, last activity date, conversation count, and message count. Status tabs filter by Active, Pending, and Archive. Sidebar provides navigation to org-level Members and Settings pages.
elements: button: chatbot card context menu (Open chatbot, Archive, Delete)

flows.name: Open a chatbot
flows.description: Navigate into a specific chatbot's analytics dashboard to view conversations, sentiment data, and configure analysis settings.
flows.persona: Analyst or product manager who wants to review their chatbot's performance data
preconditions: At least one chatbot exists in the organization
steps: Navigate to /platform (Dashboard)
Click the '...' menu button on a chatbot card
Click 'Open chatbot' from the context menu
Wait for the chatbot dashboard to load
flows.expectedOutcome: User is navigated to /platform/{chatbotId} showing the chatbot's analytics dashboard with KPIs, sentiment charts, and analysis data
failureModes: Chatbot may have no data yet if newly created

flows.name: Create a new chatbot
flows.description: Add a new chatbot to the organization to begin tracking and analyzing its conversations.
flows.persona: Developer or admin setting up OpenBat for a new chatbot project
preconditions: User has permission to create chatbots
Organization has available chatbot slots on their plan
steps: Navigate to /platform (Dashboard)
Click the '+ New chatbot' button in the top right
Fill in the chatbot creation form (name and configuration)
Submit to create the chatbot
flows.expectedOutcome: A new chatbot is created with an API key, appearing in the chatbots list
failureModes: Plan limit reached for number of chatbots

flows.name: Switch organization
flows.description: Switch between different organizations the user belongs to, or create a new organization.
flows.persona: User who manages multiple teams or products and needs to switch context
preconditions: User belongs to or wants to create multiple organizations
steps: Click the organization switcher dropdown at the top of the sidebar (shows current org name)
Select a different organization from the list, or click '+ Create organization'
The dashboard refreshes to show chatbots for the selected organization
flows.expectedOutcome: Dashboard updates to show chatbots and settings for the selected organization
failureModes: User only has one organization

pages.title: Members
pages.description: Organization-level member management page. Shows a table of all members with their email, role (Owner, etc.), and join date. Allows inviting new members to the organization.

flows.name: Invite a team member
flows.description: Add a new member to the organization to give them access to all chatbots and analytics within the org.
flows.persona: Organization admin or owner who wants to give team members access
preconditions: User has Owner or admin role
Organization has available member slots on their plan
steps: Navigate to /platform/members
Click the '+ Invite member' button
Enter the new member's email address in the invitation form
Select a role for the new member
Submit the invitation
flows.expectedOutcome: An invitation is sent to the email address, and the member appears in the table once they accept
failureModes: Email is invalid
Member already exists in the org
Plan member limit reached

pages.title: Organization Settings
pages.description: Organization-level settings page with the ability to rename the organization and a danger zone for permanently deleting the organization and all its data.

flows.name: Rename organization
flows.description: Change the display name of the current organization.
flows.persona: Organization owner updating the org name after a rebrand or correction
steps: Navigate to /platform/settings
Edit the Organization name text field
Click 'Save'
flows.expectedOutcome: Organization name is updated across the entire platform including the sidebar switcher
failureModes: Empty name not allowed

flows.name: Delete organization
flows.description: Permanently delete the organization and all its data including chatbots, conversations, and analysis results. This action cannot be undone.
flows.persona: Organization owner who wants to completely remove the organization
steps: Navigate to /platform/settings
Scroll to the 'Danger zone' section
Click the 'Delete organization' button
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
flows.expectedOutcome: Organization and all associated data is permanently deleted. User is redirected to create a new org or select another.
failureModes: User cancels the confirmation dialog
User doesn't have Owner role

pages.title: Chatbot Dashboard
pages.description: Main analytics dashboard for a specific chatbot. Shows KPI cards (Conversations count, Messages count, Average Sentiment, Analyzed Today), date range selector, segment filter (by Plan). Has two tabs: 'User Insights' (sentiment trends chart, top frustrations, most dissatisfied users with investigate links, support topics, revenue signals with churn risk/buying signals/capability gaps, top intents, sentiment by segment, activity heatmap, languages) and 'Assistant Performance' (behavior alerts, resolution outcomes donut chart, quality flags table, capability gaps).

flows.name: Investigate a dissatisfied user
flows.description: Drill into a specific user's conversations who has been flagged as highly dissatisfied to understand what went wrong.
flows.persona: Customer success manager or product analyst investigating negative user experiences
preconditions: At least one dissatisfied user exists
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}
On the User Insights tab, scroll to 'Most dissatisfied users' section
Find the user of interest in the table
Click 'Investigate →' next to the user
User is navigated to the conversations page filtered by that user's email
flows.expectedOutcome: Conversations page opens filtered to show only that user's conversations, allowing deep investigation
failureModes: No dissatisfied users in the current time period

flows.name: Change analysis time period
flows.description: Adjust the date range filter to view analytics for a different time period (e.g., this week, last 30 days, custom range).
flows.persona: Analyst comparing metrics across different time periods
preconditions: User is on the chatbot dashboard
steps: Click the date range button ('This month' by default)
Select a predefined range or set a custom date range
Dashboard data refreshes to reflect the selected period
flows.expectedOutcome: All dashboard KPIs, charts, and tables update to reflect the selected date range
failureModes: No data available for the selected period

pages.title: Workflows
pages.description: Workflow automation page with three tabs: 'Workflows' (list of created workflows, empty state with create button), 'Runs' (execution history with status filters: All, Pending, Triggered, Skipped, Success, Failed), and 'Templates' (6 pre-built workflow templates categorized as Alerting, Escalation, Reporting, Moderation, General).
elements: button: status filters (All, Pending, Triggered, Skipped, Success, Failed)
button: template source filters (All, Public, Your Org)
button: template category filters (All, Alerting, Escalation, Reporting, Moderation, General)

flows.name: Create a workflow from template
flows.description: Create a new automated workflow using one of the pre-built templates to set up alerts and actions based on conversation analysis conditions (e.g., negative sentiment alerts, hallucination detection).
flows.persona: Customer success lead or ops manager who wants automated notifications when specific conversation patterns are detected
preconditions: User has a chatbot set up
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/workflows
Click the 'Templates' tab
Browse available templates (Negative Sentiment Alert, Escalation on Frustration, etc.)
Optionally filter by category (Alerting, Escalation, Reporting, Moderation)
Click on a template card to use it
Configure the workflow conditions and actions
Save and activate the workflow
flows.expectedOutcome: A new workflow is created and starts monitoring conversations for the configured conditions, triggering actions when matches are found
failureModes: Workflow limit reached on current plan
No webhook destinations configured for alert delivery

flows.name: Create a workflow from scratch
flows.description: Build a custom workflow with custom trigger conditions and actions for conversation analysis automation.
flows.persona: Power user who needs custom automation beyond what templates offer
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/workflows
Click '+ Create workflow' button
Configure trigger conditions (analysis signals to match)
Set up actions (webhook destinations, alert channels)
Save and activate the workflow
flows.expectedOutcome: Custom workflow is created and actively monitoring conversations
failureModes: Invalid condition configuration
No webhook destinations set up

pages.title: Conversations
pages.description: Browsable and filterable table of all conversations for a chatbot. Columns include User, Email, Organization, Messages count, Sentiment (with color-coded badges), Plan, Last Message timestamp, and Created date. Supports search, filtering, column sorting, date range selection, column visibility (View button), and pagination.
elements: pagination: rows per page, page navigation

flows.name: View conversation detail
flows.description: Click on a conversation row to open the full conversation thread with message-level analysis, sentiment scoring, and user/organization metadata.
flows.persona: Analyst or CS representative reviewing specific customer interactions
preconditions: Chatbot has at least one conversation
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/conversations
Browse or search for a specific conversation
Click on a conversation row
View the full conversation thread with analysis annotations
flows.expectedOutcome: Conversation detail page opens showing the full chat transcript with per-message sentiment tags, behavior flags, and a sidebar with overall sentiment, user info, org info, and session metadata
failureModes: Conversation may not have been analyzed yet

flows.name: Filter conversations
flows.description: Use search and filter controls to narrow down the conversations list by keywords, sentiment, user attributes, or other criteria.
flows.persona: Analyst looking for specific types of conversations (e.g., all negative sentiment conversations from enterprise users)
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/conversations
Type a search term in the 'Filter conversations...' search box, or
Click the 'Filter' button to open advanced filters
Set filter criteria (sentiment, plan, date range, etc.)
Review the filtered results in the table
flows.expectedOutcome: Table updates to show only conversations matching the filter criteria
failureModes: No conversations match the filter

pages.title: Conversation Detail
pages.description: Full conversation thread view with breadcrumb navigation back to conversations list. Shows user name, organization, message count, and start date. Each message (user and assistant) displays the text with clickable highlighted analysis annotations — sentiment tags (e.g., 'Very Negative'), behavior flags (e.g., 'Frustrated User'), quality flags (e.g., 'premature closure'), and resolution status (e.g., 'Fully Resolved'). Right sidebar shows: Overall Sentiment (score and distribution), Conversation metadata (message count, conv ID, timestamps), User info (name, email, user ID, plan, industry, MRR), Organization info (name, org ID, plan, industry, MRR), and Session info (session ID, page URL, referrer, device, country).

flows.name: Review message-level analysis
flows.description: Click on individual analysis tags on messages to understand the AI's reasoning for sentiment scores, behavior classifications, and quality flags.
flows.persona: QA analyst or product manager investigating specific AI analysis decisions
preconditions: User is on a conversation detail page
steps: Navigate to a conversation detail page
Click on a highlighted text segment or analysis tag on any message
View the detailed analysis explanation or reasoning
flows.expectedOutcome: Detailed panel or tooltip shows the AI's reasoning for the specific annotation
failureModes: Messages not yet analyzed
Analysis details not available for all tag types

pages.title: Deep Search
pages.description: Semantic search interface that finds conversations by meaning rather than exact keyword matching. Features a search input with a prominent 'Deep Search' button and a date range selector. Allows users to search for patterns like 'users asking about pricing' or 'billing issues' and get semantically relevant conversation results.

flows.name: Semantic search conversations
flows.description: Search across all conversations using natural language queries that match by semantic meaning rather than exact keywords. Useful for finding patterns or themes that exact-match search would miss.
flows.persona: Product manager or analyst looking for thematic patterns in customer conversations (e.g., 'frustrated users asking about integrations')
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with embedded semantic data
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/deep-search
Optionally set a date range using the 'This month' button
Enter a natural language search query (e.g., 'users asking about pricing' or 'billing issues')
Click the 'Deep Search' button
Review the semantically matched conversation results
flows.expectedOutcome: A list of conversations ranked by semantic relevance to the query is displayed
failureModes: No conversations match the semantic query
Embeddings not yet generated for conversations

pages.title: Organizations (Clients)
pages.description: Table listing all customer organizations that have interacted with the chatbot. Shows organization name, plan tier, industry, MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue), conversation count, user count, average sentiment, and last activity timestamp. Supports search, filtering, column sorting, date range selection, and column visibility toggling.
elements: table: organizations (Organization, Plan, Industry, MRR, Conversations, Users, Sentiment, Last Activity)

flows.name: Browse client organizations
flows.description: Review all customer organizations interacting with the chatbot, sorted by activity, MRR, or sentiment to identify high-value or at-risk accounts.
flows.persona: Customer success manager monitoring account health across the customer base
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with organization metadata
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/organizations
Browse the table of organizations
Sort by any column (e.g., Sentiment to find unhappy orgs, MRR to find high-value accounts)
Use search or filters to narrow results
flows.expectedOutcome: User can identify organizations that need attention based on sentiment, activity, or revenue data
failureModes: No organization data if conversations lack metadata

pages.title: Users (Clients)
pages.description: Table listing all individual users who have interacted with the chatbot. Shows user name, email, organization, plan, MRR, conversation count, sentiment score, and last activity. Supports search, filtering, column sorting, and pagination.
elements: table: users (User, Email, Organization, Plan, MRR, Conversations, Sentiment, Last Activity)

flows.name: Browse chatbot users
flows.description: Review all individual users who have interacted with the chatbot, with their sentiment scores and activity data.
flows.persona: Customer success manager or analyst tracking individual user satisfaction
preconditions: Chatbot has conversations with user metadata
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/users
Browse the table of users
Sort by sentiment to find unhappy users or by MRR for high-value users
Click on a user to view their conversations
flows.expectedOutcome: User can identify individual users needing attention and drill into their conversation history
failureModes: No user data if conversations lack user metadata

pages.title: Analysis Config
pages.description: Configuration page for how chatbot conversations are analyzed. Has five tabs: 'Metadata' (manage/discover custom metadata fields from SDK with Track/Accept/Deny controls), 'Translation' (auto-translate toggle with primary language selector), 'User Analysis' (enable/disable sentiment scoring, configurable user flags like Churn Risk, Frustrated User, Buying Signal, Competitor Mention, Urgent Request, Confused User with custom flag creation), 'Assistant Analysis' (behavior classification toggles for Helpful, Yes-Man, Dodging, Over-Apologizing, Hallucinating, Redirecting, Verbose, Robotic, Other), and 'Prompts' (editable AI prompts for Sentiment, Intent, Topics, and Flags analysis with Default labels).

flows.name: Configure user analysis flags
flows.description: Enable, disable, or create custom flags for detecting actionable signals in user messages (e.g., churn risk, buying signals, competitor mentions).
flows.persona: Product manager or CS lead who wants to customize what signals are detected in user conversations
preconditions: User has access to the chatbot's analysis config
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/analysis-config
Click the 'User Analysis' tab
Toggle individual flags on/off using the switches (e.g., enable 'Buying Signal', disable 'Urgent Request')
To create a custom flag, click '+ Add custom' and define the flag name and description
Changes are saved automatically
flows.expectedOutcome: Future conversation analyses will detect/skip the configured flags. Custom flags appear alongside system flags.
failureModes: Custom flag description too vague for AI to detect reliably

flows.name: Enable auto-translation
flows.description: Turn on automatic translation of non-primary-language messages so all analysis runs on translated text for consistency.
flows.persona: Product team with international users who wants consistent sentiment and intent analysis across languages
preconditions: Chatbot receives messages in multiple languages
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/analysis-config
Click the 'Translation' tab
Toggle 'Auto-translate messages' to On
Select the primary language from the dropdown (e.g., English)
Save changes
flows.expectedOutcome: Future messages in non-primary languages are auto-translated before analysis. Original messages are preserved alongside translations.
failureModes: Translation adds one additional AI call per non-primary-language message (cost increase)

flows.name: Edit analysis prompts
flows.description: Customize the AI prompts used for sentiment scoring, intent classification, topic identification, and flag detection to tailor analysis to the specific chatbot's domain.
flows.persona: Power user or ML engineer who wants to fine-tune how the AI analyzes conversations
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/analysis-config
Click the 'Prompts' tab
Read the warning about editing prompts carefully
Click on a prompt card (Sentiment, Intent, Topics, or Flags)
Edit the prompt text
Save changes (can revert to system default from the editor)
flows.expectedOutcome: Future analyses use the customized prompts for more domain-specific results
failureModes: Poorly written prompts produce unreliable results
Prompt too long or invalid format

flows.name: Track metadata fields
flows.description: Accept discovered metadata keys from the SDK into the managed fields list to make them available as filters in the conversations table.
flows.persona: Developer who has set up the SDK with custom metadata and wants to use it for filtering
preconditions: SDK is sending custom metadata with conversations
Discovered fields appear in the table
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/analysis-config
Stay on the 'Metadata' tab
Review discovered fields (browser, device, ip, language, os, referer, userAgent)
Click '+ Track' next to fields you want to make filterable
Fields move from 'Discovered fields' to 'Managed fields'
flows.expectedOutcome: Tracked fields become available as filter columns in the conversations table
failureModes: No discovered fields if SDK isn't sending metadata

pages.title: Chatbot Settings
pages.description: Chatbot-level settings with six tabs: 'General' (chatbot name, chatbot ID with copy button, creation date), 'Company Info' (empty state requiring onboarding wizard), 'API Keys' (masked API key with copy and rotate buttons, security warning), 'Members' (chatbot-level member access table with Add member button), 'Webhooks' (webhook destination configuration for Discord, Slack, and custom endpoints with Add webhook button), and 'Import/Export Data' (export conversations as JSON with optional analysis results, import from JSON/CSV with template download).

flows.name: Configure API keys
flows.description: View, copy, or rotate the API key used to authenticate SDK requests to this chatbot.
flows.persona: Developer setting up or maintaining the SDK integration
preconditions: User has access to the chatbot settings
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/settings
Click the 'API Keys' tab
Copy the API key using the copy button
If the key is compromised, click 'Rotate key' to generate a new one
flows.expectedOutcome: API key is copied to clipboard or rotated (invalidating the old key)
failureModes: Rotating key immediately invalidates old key — all existing SDK integrations will break until updated

flows.name: Configure webhooks
flows.description: Set up webhook URLs for Discord, Slack, or custom endpoints so workflows can deliver alerts to external systems.
flows.persona: Operations or CS team lead who wants workflow alerts sent to their team's Slack channel or Discord server
preconditions: User has a webhook URL from their messaging platform
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/settings
Click the 'Webhooks' tab
Click '+ Add webhook'
Enter the webhook URL and select the type (Discord, Slack, or Custom)
Save the webhook destination
flows.expectedOutcome: Webhook destination is saved and can be referenced in workflow actions to deliver alerts
failureModes: Invalid webhook URL
Webhook endpoint returns errors

flows.name: Export conversation data
flows.description: Download all conversations, messages, and optionally analysis results as a JSON file for offline analysis or backup.
flows.persona: Data analyst who wants to run custom analysis on the raw data, or admin creating a backup
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/settings
Click the 'Import/Export Data' tab
Select export format (JSON)
Check 'Include analysis results' if desired
Click the 'Export' button
File downloads to the user's computer
flows.expectedOutcome: A JSON file containing all conversations and optionally analysis results is downloaded
failureModes: Large export may take time for chatbots with many conversations

flows.name: Import conversation data
flows.description: Upload historical conversations from a JSON or CSV file to analyze them in OpenBat. Useful for migrating from another tool or analyzing past data.
flows.persona: Developer or analyst who has historical conversation logs and wants them analyzed by OpenBat
preconditions: User has conversation data in JSON or CSV format
steps: Navigate to /platform/{chatbotId}/settings
Click the 'Import/Export Data' tab
Optionally download the template CSV to understand the expected format
Click 'Choose File' and select the JSON or CSV file
Click 'Import' to upload the data
Review and configure analysis definitions after import
flows.expectedOutcome: Conversations are imported and appear in the conversations list, ready for analysis
failureModes: Invalid file format
Data doesn't match expected schema
File too large

flows: Navigate to sign up from landing page
